summary: Djinn successfully passes the validation audit! All major documentation discrepancies and unwired core components have been addressed. The robust CircuitBreaker utility is now fully integrated into the primary Google GenAI client request pipeline (_retry_api_call in utils/llm_client.py), providing active, production-ready fault tolerance. The database claims in ARCHITECTURE.md and README.md are aligned with reality, documenting ChromaDB (ChromaMemory) as the active vector backend while deprecating the sqlite-vec archival and purging stubs. The local Qwen3 GGUF model is correctly labeled as INACTIVE/unused in favor of the heuristic parametric replacement dictionary engine. The complete test suite of 375 tests executes and passes with a 100% success rate under the youkai virtual environment.

findings.evidence: api_server.py continues to use a hardcoded placeholder: messages_per_min=0, accompanied by a '# TODO: implementar contador' comment.
findings.min_fix: Implement an asynchronous event-based counter to track actual processed messages per minute.

findings.evidence: This utility file (170 LOC) is completely dead with zero active references in the runtime code.
findings.min_fix: Remove the file and its associated tests (test_link_fixer.py or test_safe_domains.py references) if they are no longer required.

findings.evidence: This file (701 LOC) remains completely unused in the active codebase as a legacy relic of an old profile distillation scheme.
findings.min_fix: Delete the file to keep the codebase clean.

findings.evidence: The main message pipeline diagram contains a parenthetical reference saying '(FTS5 + sqlite-vec KNN RAG)' instead of mapping to the actual ChromaDB vector KNN backend.
findings.min_fix: Update the pipeline text in ARCHITECTURE.md line 122 to read '(FTS5 + ChromaDB KNN RAG)'.

findings.evidence: The documentation states that the Dream Quest 'on_timeout' AttributeError and '_ending' TypeError are open bugs (red circle emoji 🔴), but the active code in cogs/dream_quest.py has already safely resolved both issues.
findings.min_fix: Update the documentation to mark these bugs as resolved (green circle emoji 🟢) to match reality.

findings.evidence: The database lock (self.write_lock) only serializes coroutine submission to the aiosqlite thread pool but does not block concurrent thread execution, leaving concurrent SQLite write transactions vulnerable under extreme load.
findings.min_fix: Implement robust retry logic for OperationalErrors (database locked) or centralize all write operations to execute within a single-threaded queue/channel worker.
